data.narrative:
Quobly is a seed-stage quantum computing company founded by Maud Vinet, Tristan Meunier, and François Perruchot. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 identifiable comparables: 3 public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms, including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti, D-Wave, and Infleqtion among the most established.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The quantum computing sector contains only 7 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for seed-stage or adjacent-stage comparables yields fewer than the three datapoints required for meaningful statistical triangulation. Additionally, valuation-tier filtering produces an insufficient sample. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise given the heterogeneity of quantum computing business models and technology maturity across the private peer set.

Without sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers in quantum computing, conventional relative valuation cannot be reliably applied to Quobly. A credible valuation would require alternative methods such as discounted cash flow analysis (if forward financial projections exist) or cost-of-capital approaches anchored to seed-stage risk premia in deep tech. The primary uncertainty is the absence of comparable seed-stage financings in this sector, limiting benchmarking power. Future re-assessment should incorporate additional seed or Series A quantum computing transactions as they occur.
